name: Wok Chop Suey
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Bean sprouts, fresh or canned
1 tbsp Cornstarch dissolved in 1/3 cup water
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: Beef, shrimp, chicken, veal, or turkey may be used instead of pork.
If you are concerned about overcooking the ingredients when adding more, remove them from the wok after they are cooked and place them in a preheated oven at 150°F (65°C) until ready to combine.
1. Shred pork into narrow strips. Slice onion. Marinate both in soy sauce for a minimum of 5 minutes.
2. In a separate bowl, slice all other ingredients and arrange on a platter, ready to cook.
3. Prepare the cornstarch solution by dissolving 1 tbsp cornstarch in 1/3 cup water.
4. Heat peanut oil in the wok until hot. Arrange the cooking surface with as much oil as possible.
5. Add marinated pork and onions. Stir-fry for about 2 minutes until the pork is browned.
6. Place the lid on the wok, reduce heat to low, and cook the pork another 2-3 minutes until it is thoroughly cooked.
7. Remove the pork and onion from the wok and set aside.
8. Add bean sprouts and celery to the wok. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
9. Add all the remaining ingredients (bamboo shoots, water chestnuts, celery, mushrooms, additional bean sprouts). Season with salt and pepper to taste. Stir-fry thoroughly.
10. Return the pork and onion to the wok. Cover and cook another 2-3 minutes.
11. Remove the lid, add the cornstarch solution, and stir well before serving over rice.
